### Post by echoz on 2005-10-15
I upgrade from Hoary to Breezy via apt-get and it broke X.

Used to use the latests nVidia drivers from their site but reverted back to nvidia-glx apt-get package.

Even cleaned out my xorg.conf.

But the same error keeps happening.

Here's my xorg.conf.


### Post by McLoud on 2005-10-15
How you uninstalled the nvidia drivers? Looks like you still have the old glx library in the system, try removing nvidia-glx via apt-get, run the nvidia installer with the --uninstall option (see their README for details), then re-install nvidia-glx.

---

### Post by echoz on 2005-10-15
Tried as you suggested and I still get the same error.

People on other forums (nvnews.net) also seem to have such a problem which does suggest that Breezy broke the drivers some what.

---

### Post by metoo on 2005-10-15
Why compiling yourself. Are you aware, that breezy comes with pre-compiled kernel modules vor NVidia and ATI? If you want to use these, install 'linux-restricted-modules...' for your kernel.

But first I would delete everything in '/var/cache/apt/archives' . This way you make sure, that no older packages are on your system. Then a 'sudo apt-get install --reinstall nvidia-glx' will force a re-download of the package. So you have to be on-line.

---

### Post by echoz on 2005-10-16
Well, I tried and it still appears broken. Even went as far as to clean out the cache, clear out all the installed packages with relation to linux headers, nvidia drivers, restricted modules etc, and resintall them (re download them) from scratch through synaptic package manager and I still get a damn error of Signall 11.

Only setting the driver to "nv" gets X working. But that's without 3D accleration so its a moot point.

New error log: [http://the.ornyx.net/Xorg.1.log](http://the.ornyx.net/Xorg.1.log)
